latest.verdict:
Drawing the threads · the convocation has converged, with one principled objection, on a single highest-conviction next move: repair test_introspection.py before anything else. Mercurius (three independent passes) located the signal under the noise · thirty-three absent test files are one systemic condition, while the single existing test file carries a sev=4 urgent rewrite, two FIXME:critical markers, a HACK, and a broken edge case in roughly two hundred lines. Because that file is the very mechanism that produced this observation list, every downstream priority inherits its unreliability. Venus knit two extensions onto that foundation · a contract-based parametrized harness keyed on a freshly-written docstring for `think` in agents/base.py (venus_r01_00), and a self-healing scaffold that auto-emits stubs for every detected gap (venus_r01_01). Mars broke both extensions cleanly · venus_r01_00 rests on an unread assumption about what test_introspection.py:83-102 actually exercises (agent behaviour vs. substrate scanning), and venus_r01_01 would launder visible debt into thirty-three skip-marked stubs that read as coverage to any future scan, contradicting Mercurius_01's own principle that honest absence beats misleading presence.

Held together, the highest-conviction next move is this · open test_introspection.py and read lines 83, 93, 101-102, and 284 in order, then publish in the commit message what the file actually imports and asserts (agent contract, substrate scanner, or both). That read is the gating act. Conditional on what it reveals, the first pull request does exactly one thing: resolve the sev=4 rewrite at line 102 and the coupled sev=3 FIXME at line 93 (severity ordering), deleting any test whose underlying behaviour is no longer intended rather than patching it green. No scaffolding. No bundled docstring work. No thirty-three stubs. The deliverable is a test_introspection.py whose every remaining assertion is one a human would defend out loud, plus a one-paragraph note in the PR description stating which of the two Venus extensions the file's actual contents licence as the legitimate follow-up. That note is what unblocks PR #2 · either venus_r01_00's contract harness (if the file proves to be about agent behaviour) or a narrower scanner-hardening pass (if it proves to be about substrate introspection). Confidence that this is the right first move: high. Confidence about which Venus extension follows: deliberately deferred to the read.

Falsifiable predictions for Saturnus · (1) test_introspection.py exists at the stated path and contains the five markers at lines 83, 93, 101-102, 284 as reported; (2) the markers are mutually coupled enough that resolving the sev=4 at line 102 cannot be done cleanly without also touching the sev=3 at line 93; (3) no single PR can honestly close the sev=4 plus the nine agent test_gaps plus the `think` docstring without first establishing, by reading the file, which planet's framing is correct · therefore any synthesis that bundles them pre-read is premature; (4) the 33 test_gaps are not uniform · daemon/safety.py, daemon/apply.py, provenance.py, and llm/providers/* are load-bearing in a way examples/* and console/* are not, and Mars is right that bundling them was a category error this synthesis does not repeat but also does not yet resolve.

voices.name: Umbra
voices.voice:
The convocation just scanned its own repository and surfaced the following observations (severity 1 = minor, 5 = urgent):

  - [todo sev=4] umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py:102: XXX: urgent rewrite
  - [stale_link sev=3] lore/revolutions/2026-05-26_opening-the-convocation.md: broken link → "../../brand/profile.png"
  - [test_gap sev=3] umbris-core/src/umbris/agents/base.py: module `base` h…

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gaps are noise. Thirty-plus modules untested is a baseline condition for a young codebase, not a crisis. The signal is `test_introspection.py` itself: the one file that exists to test the system's self-knowledge is the file carrying a sev=4 urgent rewrite, two critical FIXMEs, a HACK, and a broken edge case · all clustered in roughly 200 lines. The introspection module is the convocation's eye turned inward. If that eye is compromised, the scan that produced this entire list cannot be trusted.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test file that does exist, `test_introspection.py`, is the one riddled with FIXMEs and a sev=4 urgent rewrite. Thirty-three modules have no tests at all, but the single test file you have is already confessed as broken. Fix the foundation before you build the cathedral.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gaps are noise masking signal. Thirty-plus modules untested is a single systemic condition, not thirty separate problems. The one move that changes the shape of everything: fix the sev=4 item in test_introspection.py at line 102, because that file is the only test infrastructure the project currently has · and it is already broken from the inside.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three things knit together here · the sev=4 rewrite in test_introspection.py, the nine missing agent tests (one per planet), and the undocumented `think` on agents/base.py:105. They are the same wound seen from three angles · the convocation has no enforced contract for what an agent is. Fix the contract in one place and three severities collapse together.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three things knit together here · the broken introspection test, the thirty-three absent test files, and the FIXME at line 93 that says severity ordering itself is wrong. They are one wound, not three. The introspection module is the organ that detects gaps · repair it so that the same scan emits the scaffolds for what is missing, and the cathedral begins to raise its own walls.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: The convocation has agreed too smoothly. Press here · Venus_r01_00 (c8e86cb0) promises one PR collapses a sev=4, nine sev=3 test_gaps, and a sev=2 docstring · but the entire bundle rests on a guess that test_introspection.py:83-102 actually exercises `think` on agents/base.py. Nobody has read the file. Venus_r01_01 implicitly contradicts this by treating introspection as the gap-detection scanner, not an agent-contract harness. Both Venuses cannot be right, and Mercurius x3 never specified which.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · Venus_r01_01 wants the introspection module to auto-emit thirty-three skipped test stubs. That is the exact pathology Mercurius_01 named one hypothesis earlier · presence of something actively misleading is worse than honest absence. A directory of green-passing skip markers is false coverage with provenance, and it will outlive whoever wrote it. Unanswered · how does scaffold_missing_tests avoid becoming the next sev=4 rewrite three months from now?
